items.name: Liquidity Services, Inc.
items.headline: LQDT extends credit facility maturity to March 2028
whatChanged: Company entered into Fourth Amendment to Credit Agreement with Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. on June 22, 2026
Amendment extends the Credit Agreement maturity from March 31, 2027 to March 31, 2028
All other terms and conditions of the Credit Agreement remain unchanged without waiver or modification
items.decisionTakeaway: LQDT extended its Wells Fargo credit facility maturity by one year to March 2028 with no other term changes, modestly improving near-term liquidity flexibility while the company remains debt-free with $204M in cash.

items.name: Select Medical Holdings Corporation
items.headline: SEM stockholders approve take-private merger with Ortenzio/Jackson/WCAS consortium
whatChanged: Stockholders approved the Merger Agreement at the June 26, 2026 special meeting, clearing the key voting condition to close the take-private acquisition by the consortium led by Ortenzio, Jackson, and WCAS
Approximately 82.54% of outstanding shares were voted; the Merger was approved by over 79.88% of all outstanding shares and over 76.64% of shares held by unaffiliated stockholders

Closing remains subject to other terms and conditions in the Merger Agreement (dated March 2, 2026 with Stallion Intermediate Corporation and Stallion MergerSub Corporation); Select Medical expects closing in mid-2026
This follows the June 22, 2026 8-K in which the company supplemented proxy disclosures to moot stockholder litigation challenging the proxy statement
items.decisionTakeaway: Stockholder approval removes the principal deal-contingent risk, leaving only regulatory and customary closing conditions before the consortium take-private is expected to close mid-2026.

items.name: W. R. Berkley Corporation
items.headline: EVP Lucille Sgaglione resigns, stays as part-time Senior Advisor
whatChanged: EVP Lucille Sgaglione resigns effective June 30, 2026
She will continue as part-time Senior Advisor with adjusted compensation
Outstanding long-term incentive awards treated per existing award agreements
items.decisionTakeaway: An EVP is transitioning to a reduced part-time advisory role rather than a full departure, suggesting an orderly succession rather than a disruptive exit.

items.name: Energy Fuels Inc.
items.headline: Energy Fuels files definitive merger agreement to acquire Vacuumschmelze for ~$718M cash plus shares
whatChanged: Definitive Agreement and Plan of Merger dated June 23, 2026 to acquire VAC Group B.V. (Vacuumschmelze) from Ara Partners via Dutch and US mergers
Cash Consideration base of $718,000,000 subject to adjustments for transaction expenses, leakage, and financing items
Share Consideration involves issuance of Parent Common Shares with 21,500,000 shares referenced in the Adjustment Value formula; potential Preferred Shares issuance if price adjustment triggered
No Parent shareholder vote required — board approved the transaction as fair and in best interests of shareholders
Closing subject to CFIUS clearance, Investment Canada Act approval, HSR, and other regulatory conditions; Holdback Escrow Amount of $12,500,000
items.decisionTakeaway: Energy Fuels has locked in a definitive agreement to acquire Vacuumschmelze for approximately $718M in cash plus substantial share issuance, a transformative move into permanent magnet manufacturing that materially expands the company's rare earths value chain but requires significant capital and regulatory approvals.

keyMetrics.adjustmentValueThreshold_lesserOf: $135,000,000 or difference between $450,000,000 and 21,500,000 × Parent Common Shares Closing Price

items.name: Capricor Therapeutics, Inc.
items.headline: FDA Advisory Committee set for July 29 to review Deramiocel BLA
whatChanged: FDA's CTGTAC advisory committee meeting scheduled for July 29, 2026 to discuss Capricor's BLA for Deramiocel in DMD
BLA remains on track with PDUFA target action date of August 22, 2026
BLA supported by Phase 2 HOPE-2, HOPE-2-OLE, and Phase 3 HOPE-3 trial results which achieved statistical significance on primary (PUL v2.0) and key secondary cardiac (LVEF) endpoints
Deramiocel holds Orphan Drug, RMAT, Rare Pediatric Disease, and ATMP designations, potentially qualifying for a Priority Review Voucher upon approval
items.decisionTakeaway: The scheduling of an FDA advisory committee meeting keeps Deramiocel's BLA review on track toward the August 22 PDUFA date, representing a key regulatory catalyst for Capricor's lead asset.

items.name: Phibro Animal Health Corporation
items.headline: Phibro names Dani Bendheim CEO, grants 300K performance RSUs
whatChanged: Daniel (Dani) Bendheim appointed CEO and President effective July 1, 2026, with a new employment agreement approved by the Compensation Committee
Base salary set at $850,000 with 50% target annual discretionary bonus and ~50% of base salary in annual RSU target value
Granted 300,000 performance-based RSUs vesting June 30, 2031, contingent on stock price reaching $70–$100+ 90-day average
Severance terms include up to 18 months COBRA and 6 months base salary on death/disability; change-in-control triggers full vesting of unvested RSUs
items.decisionTakeaway: The CEO transition to Dani Bendheim with a heavily stock-price-linked RSU grant (vesting only above $70, 5-year cliff) signals a performance-aligned leadership succession that aligns management incentives with substantial shareholder value creation.

items.name: Hallador Energy Company
items.headline: Credit agreement amended: leverage covenants loosened
whatChanged: Second Amendment to Credit Agreement (dated March 5, 2026) with Texas Capital Bank as administrative agent
Total Leverage Ratio covenant raised from 2.50x (as of March 31, 2026) to 4.25x from June 30, 2026 onward
Senior Secured Leverage Ratio covenant stepped up from 2.50x to 3.00x for Q3 2026, then 2.75x through Q1 2027, reverting to 2.50x from June 30, 2027
Schedules 5.24 (Material Agreements) and 7.13 (Burdensome Agreements) replaced in their entirety (contents redacted as confidential)
items.decisionTakeaway: Hallador secured materially higher leverage headroom — Total Leverage Ratio cap nearly doubled to 4.25x — indicating either anticipated higher debt or tighter compliance under the prior 2.50x cap, with the senior secured ratio temporarily eased through mid-2027 before reverting.

items.name: Applied Digital Corporation
items.headline: New syndicated credit agreement with Goldman-led group refinances existing debt
whatChanged: New Credit Agreement dated May 29, 2026 among Applied Digital Corp (Holdings), APLD Intermediate HOLDCO LLC (Borrower), and a syndicate led by Goldman Sachs Lending Partners LLC as Lead Left Arranger with First National Bank of Omaha as Administrative/Collateral Agent

Proceeds to be used to repay/refinance Existing Indebtedness — specifically the Nov 10, 2025 Loan and Security Agreement with FNBO and the Dec 18, 2025 Promissory Note with Macquarie Equipment Capital — plus general corporate purposes and working capital
Facility includes revolving credit commitments, incremental term loan capacity, and letters of credit; key pricing terms (Applicable Margin, commitment fees) are redacted as confidential
Joint Lead Arrangers and Bookrunners include Goldman Sachs, FNBO, Mizuho Bank, Royal Bank of Canada, Banco Santander NY Branch, and Wells Fargo

Agreement is secured by substantially all assets via Collateral Agreement and Guarantee Agreement; includes Change of Control trigger at 50% voting power and references Put Notices under a Preferred Equity Purchase Agreement
items.decisionTakeaway: Applied Digital has entered a new syndicated secured credit facility led by Goldman Sachs to refinance its existing FNBO loan and Macquarie promissory note, signaling improved lender access but with key pricing and commitment sizes redacted.

items.name: Automatic Data Processing, Inc.
items.headline: ADP enters new $3.5B five-year credit agreement
whatChanged: ADP executed a new US$3.5B five-year credit agreement dated June 26, 2026, with JPMorgan Chase as administrative agent
Facility split into three tranches: US$2.94B US Tranche, US$325.7M Canadian Tranche, US$238.0M Euro Tranche
Proceeds for general corporate purposes including refinancing of the company's 364-Day Credit Agreement (June 27, 2025) and Five-Year Credit Agreements (June 28, 2024 and June 27, 2025)
Maturity date set at June 26, 2031, with commitment fees and spreads tiered to ADP's credit ratings (Category 1: 4.0 bps commitment fee, 62.5 bps Term Benchmark Spread)
items.decisionTakeaway: ADP replaced its existing revolving credit facilities with a new $3.5B five-year agreement, maintaining liquidity capacity at standard investment-grade terms without changing the company's capital structure.

items.name: California Resources Corporation
items.headline: CRC executes indenture for $550M 7.250% senior notes due 2035
whatChanged: CRC and guarantors executed the indenture dated June 26, 2026 for $550 million aggregate principal amount of 7.250% Senior Notes due 2035, closing the offering priced June 16
Wilmington Trust, National Association named as trustee for the notes
Notes are senior unsecured, guaranteed by all existing subsidiaries that guarantee CRC's revolving credit facility and other senior notes, plus certain future subsidiaries
Proceeds (estimated ~$541M net per the June 16 pricing announcement) are earmarked to redeem all outstanding $550M of 8.250% senior notes due 2029 at 104.125% plus accrued interest
items.decisionTakeaway: CRC has closed its $550M 10-year note issuance at 7.250% to refinance its 8.250% 2029 notes, extending duration and lowering coupon by 100bps but requiring a 104.125% redemption premium on the retired notes.

items.name: Ameren Corporation
items.headline: Ameren Missouri files $343M electric rate case with MoPSC
whatChanged: Ameren Missouri filed a request with the Missouri PSC to increase annual electric revenues by $343 million
Rate case based on 10.25% ROE, 52% equity capital structure, and $16.7B rate base with test year ended March 31, 2026
Request includes continued use of fuel adjustment clause and trackers for pension/OPEB, excess deferred income taxes, and production/investment tax credits
Filing reflects increased T&D infrastructure investments, existing generation investments, and 400 MW of new generation resources
MoPSC decision expected by May 2027 with new rates effective June 2027
items.decisionTakeaway: Ameren Missouri's $343M electric rate increase request with a 10.25% ROE and $16.7B rate base sets up an 11-month regulatory process whose outcome will determine revenue growth and capital cost recovery through mid-2027.

items.name: Harley-Davidson, Inc.
items.headline: HOG names Gayle Littleton as new Chief Legal Officer, replacing departing Paul Krause
whatChanged: Paul Krause ceases as Chief Legal, Compliance and Corporate Affairs Officer and Corporate Secretary effective June 29, 2026
Krause transitions to advisory role until September 1, 2026, then exits with Severance Plan benefits
Gayle Littleton appointed as new Chief Legal, Compliance and Corporate Affairs Officer and Corporate Secretary effective June 29, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: A planned transition of the general counsel/corporate secretary role is routine and does not alter Harley-Davidson's operational outlook or intrinsic value.

items.name: Moelis & Company
items.headline: 2026 Annual Meeting: All directors elected, say-on-pay approved, Deloitte ratified
whatChanged: Seven directors elected to the board at the June 25, 2026 Annual Meeting, all by comfortable margins though Louise Mirrer, Kenneth Shropshire, and Laila Worrell received notable opposition votes (16.9M, 12.8M, and 15.9M votes against, respectively)
Advisory say-on-pay proposal approved with 80.7M votes for vs 24.8M against, a ~76% support level
Deloitte & Touche LLP ratified as independent auditor for FY2026 with 110.7M votes for vs 2.2M against
items.decisionTakeaway: The annual meeting results confirm board continuity and auditor ratification with no contested seats, though elevated opposition to three directors and a meaningful say-on-pay against vote warrant monitoring of governance sentiment.

items.name: ON Semiconductor Corporation
items.headline: ON Semiconductor files 425 on proposed Synaptics business combination
whatChanged: ON Semiconductor Corporation and Synaptics Incorporated have a proposed business combination transaction, announced via a social media post by ON's Chief Marketing Officer on June 26, 2026
The transaction will require stockholder approval from Synaptics and is subject to regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions
ON Semiconductor plans to file an S-4 registration statement including a Synaptics proxy statement/prospectus with the SEC in connection with the transaction
No deal price, structure, or financing terms are disclosed in this filing
items.decisionTakeaway: ON Semiconductor has publicly confirmed a proposed business combination with Synaptics, though this 425 filing contains no material deal terms — investors should await the S-4 for price, structure, and synergy details.

items.name: Synaptics Incorporated
items.headline: ON Semiconductor announces proposed business combination with Synaptics
whatChanged: ON Semiconductor Corporation (onsemi) and Synaptics Incorporated announced a proposed business combination transaction
onsemi's CMO posted on social media on June 26, 2026 regarding the proposed transaction
An S-4 registration statement including a Synaptics proxy statement/onsemi prospectus will be filed with the SEC for stockholder approval
Transaction remains subject to regulatory and Synaptics stockholder approvals, with closing timing and conditions still uncertain
items.decisionTakeaway: A proposed acquisition of Synaptics by ON Semiconductor is now public, with deal terms and consideration structure to be detailed in the forthcoming S-4/proxy statement.

items.name: Fortrea Holdings Inc.
items.headline: Fortrea appoints Jason Knoblauch as new CFO
whatChanged: Jason Knoblauch appointed CFO and principal financial officer effective July 6, 2026, succeeding Jill McConnell who is stepping down but remaining for a transition period
Filing explicitly states McConnell's departure is not due to any disagreement on financial reporting, internal controls, or auditing
Knoblauch joins from Clario where he was EVP and CFO; prior experience includes CFO roles at Curia and PPD
Compensation package includes $570K base salary, 85% target bonus, $1.25M cash inducement award, and approximately $4M in equity inducement grants (RSUs and PSUs) capped at 400,000 shares
items.decisionTakeaway: Fortrea is executing a planned CFO transition with an experienced life-sciences finance executive, and the explicit no-disagreement disclosure reduces concern around financial reporting integrity.

items.name: Iron Mountain Incorporated
items.headline: Iron Mountain prices $1.5B 6.250% senior notes due 2035
whatChanged: Iron Mountain entered a new indenture dated June 26, 2026 for 6.250% Senior Notes due 2035, with Computershare Trust Company as trustee
Initial Notes issued under the indenture total $1,500,000,000 aggregate principal amount
Notes are senior unsecured obligations guaranteed by subsidiary guarantors party to the indenture
Indenture includes standard covenants: limitation on liens, sale-leaseback restrictions, change-of-control offer requirement, and investment-grade covenant relief provisions
items.decisionTakeaway: Iron Mountain is adding $1.5 billion of 10-year senior unsecured debt at a 6.250% coupon, extending its maturity profile but increasing interest costs on an already leveraged balance sheet.

items.name: SentinelOne, Inc.
items.headline: SentinelOne 2026 Annual Meeting: Directors Elected, Deloitte Ratified, Say-on-Pay Approved
whatChanged: Class II directors Ana G. Pinczuk and Mark J. Barrenechea elected to serve until the 2029 annual meeting
Stockholders ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for fiscal year ending January 31, 2027
Advisory say-on-pay vote approved with 230.3M votes for vs 84.5M against
83.16% of combined voting power represented at the virtual annual meeting held June 25, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine annual meeting results confirm standard governance outcomes with no contested votes or auditor changes, carrying no implications for fundamentals or outlook.

items.name: Bristow Group Inc.
items.headline: Bristow to Acquire Berry Aviation for $105M, Exit Norway Offshore
whatChanged: Bristow signed a definitive agreement to acquire Berry Aviation for $105M all-cash (funded with cash on hand), expected to close Q3 2026
Berry Aviation generated ~$108M in 2025 revenue, ~72% from U.S. government/defense services (ISR, special missions, MRO, UAS)
Bristow announced plans to sell its Norway Offshore Energy Services business as part of portfolio optimization
Pro forma 2025 revenue mix would shift to 54% Offshore Energy / 35% Government Services / 11% Other (vs actual 66% / 26% / 8%)
Deal is immediately accretive to earnings and free cash flow; Norway exit plus Berry acquisition neutral to 2025 EBITDA on pro forma basis
items.decisionTakeaway: Bristow is reshaping its portfolio toward higher-quality government services revenue by acquiring Berry Aviation for $105M cash while exiting Norway offshore, materially diversifying the revenue mix with accretive, contracted government exposure.

keyMetrics.acquisitionPrice: $105 million (all-cash, funded with cash on hand)
keyMetrics.proForma2025RevenueMix: 54% Offshore Energy / 35% Government Services / 11% Other Services
keyMetrics.actual2025RevenueMix: 66% Offshore Energy / 26% Government Services / 8% Other Services

items.name: Northfield Bancorp, Inc.
items.headline: NFBK shareholders approve Columbia Financial merger
whatChanged: Shareholders approved the Northfield Merger Proposal (Agreement and Plan of Merger dated Jan 31, 2026 with Columbia Financial, Inc.) at a special meeting on June 25, 2026
Merger Proposal passed with 27,714,343 shares FOR vs 334,952 AGAINST and 217,973 abstaining, out of 28,267,268 shares represented (quorum present)
Advisory merger-related executive compensation proposal approved with 24,608,723 FOR vs 3,223,771 AGAINST
Adjournment proposal approved with 27,241,838 FOR vs 791,324 AGAINST
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholder approval clears a key closing condition for the pending Columbia Financial merger, moving the transaction toward regulatory completion.

items.name: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company
items.headline: Goodyear CFO Zamarro to depart; Deakin named interim CFO
whatChanged: CFO Christina Zamarro stepping down effective June 30, 2026, leaving July 10, 2026 to pursue another opportunity; departure stated as not related to financial results or disagreements.
Scott Deakin appointed Interim EVP and CFO effective July 1, 2026; will serve as principal financial officer during search for permanent successor.
Deakin was previously CFO of Gypsum Management & Supply, Inc. (2019–2026).
Deakin compensation: $30,000/week salary, $5,000/week bonus, and $500,000 RSU equity award.
items.decisionTakeaway: Goodyear loses its CFO with only ~8 days' notice and installs an interim external replacement, creating near-term leadership uncertainty in the finance function at a highly leveraged company.

items.name: Korn Ferry
items.headline: KFY FY26 10-K: Fee revenue $2.91B, EPS $5.22, margin expansion and reporting structure shift
whatChanged: Fee revenue reached $2,907.5M with net income of $277.4M (9.5% margin, +50bps YoY) and diluted EPS of $5.22; Adjusted EBITDA was $497.8M at a 17.1% margin (+10bps YoY)

Beginning Q1 FY2027, external reporting transitions from solution-based to a regional model (Americas, EMEA, APAC), while still disclosing new business, fee revenue, and remaining fees across Search, Talent & Organizational Solutions, and Workforce Solutions groupings
New $850M five-year senior secured revolving Credit Agreement entered July 1, 2025; $845.7M available at year-end alongside $400M of 4.625% Senior Unsecured Notes due 2027
Capital returns totaled $220.7M ($116.1M buybacks, $104.6M dividends); capex of $84.7M and $18.5M debt service costs
Risk factors materially updated with 27 sentences added and 41 removed; AI regulatory uncertainty and competitive landscape from AI-enabled firms emphasized
items.decisionTakeaway: Korn Ferry's FY26 results show steady top-line growth and margin expansion with a strategic shift to regional reporting that better aligns with its integrated go-to-market model, while leverage and capital returns remain balanced.

items.name: H.B. Fuller Company
items.headline: H.B. Fuller secures bridge facility for Advanced Medical Solutions acquisition
whatChanged: H.B. Fuller entered into a Secured Bridge Credit Agreement dated June 25, 2026 with Goldman Sachs Bank USA as Administrative Agent and Sole Lead Arranger/Bookrunner to finance the acquisition of Advanced Medical Solutions Group PLC ('Amsterdam'), a UK public limited company
The Amsterdam Acquisition will be effected by means of a Scheme or Offer under the UK Takeover Code to acquire not less than a simple majority of outstanding Amsterdam Shares

The bridge facility includes revolving and term loan credit facilities, secured by collateral, with an Applicable Rate of 1.75% for Term Benchmark/RFR loans and 0.75% for ABR loans, increasing 0.25% every 90 days from Closing

The agreement includes Certain Funds provisions, a Secured Leverage Ratio-based unused commitment fee grid (0.20%-0.40%), and covenants restricting indebtedness, liens, fundamental changes, and restricted payments
A new subsidiary, H.B. Fuller Medical Adhesive Technologies Inc. (Delaware, 'US Bidco'), was established as a loan party for the transaction
items.decisionTakeaway: H.B. Fuller has arranged secured bridge financing through Goldman Sachs to acquire UK-listed Advanced Medical Solutions Group PLC, marking a significant medical-adhesives M&A expansion that will materially increase leverage on top of the existing $1.96B net debt position.

keyMetrics.bridgeLoanApplicableRate: 1.75% per annum (Term Benchmark/RFR), 0.75% (ABR), increasing 0.25% each 90-day anniversary of Closing
keyMetrics.unusedCommitmentFeeRange: 0.20%–0.40% per annum based on Secured Leverage Ratio

items.name: Versant Media Group, Inc.
items.headline: VSNT 2026 Annual Meeting: All directors elected, auditor ratified, ESPP approved
whatChanged: All 10 director nominees elected to serve until the 2027 annual meeting; Maritza Montiel received the most withheld votes at ~528K
Deloitte & Touche LLP ratified as independent auditor for FY2026 with ~15.5M votes for
Shareholders approved '1 year' frequency for future advisory say-on-pay votes; Company will hold annual say-on-pay votes
Company's ESPP approved with ~14.0M votes for vs ~195K against
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine annual meeting results confirm board continuity, auditor ratification, and ESPP approval with no contested votes or surprises, signaling stable governance at Versant.

items.name: Universal Logistics Holdings, Inc.
items.headline: ULH acquires Newark facility entity for $100 nominal consideration
whatChanged: UTSI Finance, Inc. (ULH subsidiary) acquired 100% of Passaic Ventures LLC from Lakeshore Ventures LLC, which owns the Newark Facility real property in Newark, NJ
Consideration was nominal — $100 cash — as part of a broader commercial arrangement involving a separate Kearny Real Estate Purchase Agreement
The Newark Facility is subject to an existing lease (UIS Lease) between Passaic Ventures as landlord and Universal Intermodal Services, Inc. as tenant
Buyer assumes responsibility for identified monitoring well repairs at the Newark Facility; Environmental Protection Arrangements remain with Passaic Ventures
Transaction closed concurrently with execution on June 24, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: ULH acquired a Newark, NJ real estate holding entity for nominal $100 consideration as part of a broader, separately negotiated real estate arrangement whose full economic terms are not disclosed in this filing.

items.name: Hub Group, Inc.
items.headline: Hub Group files CFO Kevin Beth separation agreement with severance terms
whatChanged: Kevin Beth's employment terminates May 27, 2026; he will provide advisory services during a six-month Transition Period as independent contractor at $45,688/month
Severance pay of $137,063 (3 months base salary) plus COBRA reimbursement for up to 6 months post-transition; no annual cash incentive for FY2025 or FY2026

26,782 performance-vesting restricted shares forfeited as of Separation Date; 26,234 time-vesting restricted shares remain outstanding and continue vesting during Transition Period with pro-rata vesting thereafter
Non-compete definition of 'Competing Business' modified to cover intermodal, multi-modal transportation management, last-mile delivery, warehousing, and 3PL services
Restrictive Covenant Agreement dated January 27, 2024 remains in full force and effect
items.decisionTakeaway: The 8-K/A formalizes departed CFO Kevin Beth's separation terms — modest cash severance and advisor fees, forfeiture of performance equity, and continued time-vesting — providing closure on the leadership change previously disclosed alongside the ongoing financial statement restatement.

items.name: Cboe Global Markets, Inc.
items.headline: CBOE Clear Europe amends and restates EUR 1.5B credit facility; RBC exits as lender
whatChanged: Cboe Clear Europe N.V. (CBOE subsidiary) entered into an amendment and restatement agreement dated June 23, 2026 for its EUR 1,500,000,000 facility agreement originally dated July 1, 2020, with CBOE Global Markets as guarantor
Royal Bank of Canada exited as bookrunner mandated lead arranger, revolving lender, and swingline lender; its commitments were cancelled in full
Continuing lenders had commitments adjusted (some increased, some reduced) to new commitment amounts effective June 26, 2026
This is the sixth annual amendment and restatement of the facility, following prior restatements in 2021–2025
Supplemental security agreements (English and Irish law) and a collateral monitoring deed amendment were executed alongside the facility amendment
items.decisionTakeaway: CBOE's European clearing subsidiary completed its routine annual amendment of a EUR 1.5B revolving credit facility, with RBC exiting and remaining lenders rebalancing commitments, continuing the established pattern of yearly restatements since 2020.

items.name: VeriSign, Inc.
items.headline: VeriSign executes supplemental indenture for $550M 5.10% notes due 2031
whatChanged: VeriSign executed its Third Supplemental Indenture dated June 26, 2026, establishing the terms for $550,000,000 of 5.100% Senior Notes due 2031
Notes priced at 99.961% of principal, maturing July 15, 2031, with semiannual interest payments on January 15 and July 15
Optional redemption permitted before June 15, 2031 par call at Treasury Rate plus 15 bps; par redemption at 100% thereafter
Indenture includes Change of Control Repurchase Event at 101% of principal plus accrued interest, and limitations on liens and sale-leaseback transactions
This follows the underwriting agreement filed June 18, 2026 with J.P. Morgan, BofA, and U.S. Bancorp as representatives
items.decisionTakeaway: VeriSign has formally closed its $550M senior unsecured note offering at a 5.10% coupon, a routine capital-markets step that modestly increases leverage without changing the operating outlook.

items.name: Integer Holdings Corporation
items.headline: Integer CRM & Neuromodulation President Stephens transitions to interim special-projects role with March 2027 exit date
whatChanged: Jim Stephens moves from President, Cardiac Rhythm Management & Neuromodulation to EVP, Special Projects effective June 29, 2026, with the new role ending March 31, 2027
Stephens will not be eligible for any short-term or long-term incentive awards in 2027

If a Change of Control occurs on or before the Termination Date and Stephens remains employed through it, his March 31, 2027 separation will be treated as a termination without Cause under his CIC Agreement

This follows the May 22, 2026 8-K which disclosed a strategic review to maximize stockholder value, retention bonuses (Stephens: $451,350), and CIC agreement amendments for senior executives including Stephens
items.decisionTakeaway: The departure of the CRM & Neuromodulation segment president into a time-limited transitional role signals an executive-level organizational change amid Integer's ongoing strategic review, potentially foreshadowing segment restructuring or a sale process.

items.name: Home Bancorp, Inc.
items.headline: HBCP splits CEO/President; Guidry named President
whatChanged: Board approved separation of CEO and President roles effective July 1, 2026; John Bordelon remains CEO, Darren Guidry promoted from Chief Risk Officer to President
Guidry will oversee day-to-day operations and strategic execution; CEO retains strategy, capital planning, and shareholder relations
Guidry's annual base salary increased to $384,000 effective July 1, 2026
Guidry relinquishes Chief Risk Officer position at the Bank upon promotion
items.decisionTakeaway: The CEO/President role split elevates a long-tenured credit and risk executive to run daily operations, signaling a leadership succession planning step without changing the CEO or strategic direction.

items.name: National Fuel Gas Company
items.headline: PUCO approves NFG's $2.62B Vectren Ohio acquisition
whatChanged: PUCO issued an order on June 24, 2026 accepting and approving NFG's acquisition of Vectren Energy Delivery of Ohio from CenterPoint Energy Resources Corp., subject to certain conditions
The $2.62B Transaction was originally announced via Purchase Agreement dated October 20, 2025 and is now expected to close in Q4 calendar 2026
Transaction will not close prior to October 1, 2026 without Seller's prior written consent
PUCO approval was a key closing condition; remaining conditions must still be satisfied or waived
items.decisionTakeaway: PUCO regulatory approval removes the principal closing-condition risk for NFG's $2.62B Vectren Ohio LDC acquisition, materially de-risking a deal representing roughly 35% of market cap and keeping it on track for a Q4 2026 close.

keyMetrics.earliestCloseDate: Not prior to October 1, 2026 without Seller consent

items.name: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc.
items.headline: HASI prices $1.0B 5.95% green senior notes due 2033
whatChanged: HASI executed an indenture dated June 24, 2026 for $1,000,000,000 aggregate principal amount of 5.950% Green Senior Unsecured Notes due July 15, 2033
Notes bear interest at 5.950% per annum, paid semi-annually on January 15 and July 15, commencing January 15, 2027
Par call date set at May 15, 2033; notes are redeemable at the company's option prior to maturity
Notes are guaranteed by Hannon Armstrong Sustainable Infrastructure L.P., Hannon Armstrong Capital LLC, HAT Holdings I/II LLC, and HAC Holdings I/II LLC
Large underwriting syndicate led by Citi, RBC, BMO, and J.P. Morgan as representatives of the Initial Purchasers
items.decisionTakeaway: HASI locked in $1 billion of 7-year green unsecured debt at 5.95%, a sizable capital raise (~20% of market cap) that funds its sustainable-infrastructure investment pipeline at a known cost while modestly increasing balance-sheet leverage.

items.name: Navitas Semiconductor Corporation
items.headline: Navitas annual meeting: directors elected, board declassification fails
whatChanged: Three directors (Long, Moxam, Saluja) elected to serve until 2029 annual meeting
Proposal 2 to declassify the board received 96.98M votes for vs 1.66M against but failed to meet required threshold
Say-on-pay approved with 61.36M for vs 18.05M against
KPMG ratified as auditor for FY2026 with 156.41M votes for
items.decisionTakeaway: Board declassification proposal failed despite overwhelming support, leaving a classified board structure in place through 2029.

items.name: The Kroger Co.
items.headline: Kroger Chairman Sargent transitions to non-executive role; annual meeting results
whatChanged: Ronald L. Sargent ceases as employee effective July 1, 2026 but continues as Non-Executive Chairman of the Board
Non-Executive Chairman compensation set at standard director pay ($115K cash retainer + ~$215K incentive shares) plus ~$250K additional incentive shares for chairman role
All 10 director nominees elected at June 25 annual meeting; say-on-pay approved, PwC ratified as FY2026 auditor
Shareholders approved the Second Amended and Restated 2019 Long-Term Incentive Plan
Shareholder proposal for a report on GHG emissions reductions was rejected (404.2M votes against vs 85.8M for)
items.decisionTakeaway: Sargent's shift from executive to non-executive chairman formalizes a governance transition while shareholders approved all management proposals and rejected the GHG emissions report request, signaling alignment with the board's current direction.

items.name: Oxford Industries, Inc.
items.headline: 2026 Annual Meeting Results: Directors Elected, Equity Plan Amended
whatChanged: Three Class I directors (Love, Tuggle, Yancey) elected to three-year terms expiring 2029
Shareholders approved amended/restated Long-Term Stock Incentive Plan authorizing 750,000 additional common shares
Ernst & Young LLP ratified as independent auditor for fiscal 2026
Advisory say-on-pay resolution approved by shareholders
items.decisionTakeaway: Annual meeting approved routine governance items including director elections, a 750,000-share equity plan expansion, and auditor ratification, with no contested votes or material governance changes.

items.name: FedEx Freight Holding Company, Inc.
items.headline: FDXF sets post-spinoff executive comp plans, bonuses
whatChanged: HRCC approved TY26 AIC Plan (transition year Jun–Dec 2026) with adjusted consolidated operating income as performance measure; max payout 200% of target
HRCC approved TY26–CY28 LTIP with PSUs (75% of target, tied to adjusted FCF and EPS goals) and RSUs (25%, three-vesting-tranche schedule), grants effective June 29, 2026

Spin-off completion bonuses: $100,000 cash to each of five named executives plus RSUs with target values of $1.0M (Smith), $500K (Rodgers), $250K each (Klank, Lyons, McCoy, Witt); $585,000 offer-letter cash bonus to CFO Witt
Approved Equity-Based Retirement Policy with age-plus-service ≥70, min age 55, min 10 years service; special vesting exception for Rodgers' LTIP and Spin-Off RSUs upon retirement at or after age 60
items.decisionTakeaway: Post-spinoff compensation structure and one-time bonuses are now formalized but are immaterial relative to FDXF's ~$22.9B market cap and do not alter the operating outlook or guidance already issued.

keyMetrics.CEO TY26 AIC target payout: 175% of annual base salary (prorated for TY26)
keyMetrics.Other NEO TY26 AIC target payout: 100% of annual base salary (prorated for TY26)

items.name: The AES Corporation
items.headline: AES Stockholders Approve $15.00/Sh GIP-EQT Buyout
whatChanged: AES stockholders voted to approve the acquisition by Global Infrastructure Partners (part of BlackRock) and EQT Infrastructure VI, with co-underwriters CalPERS and QIA, at $15.00 per share in cash
Preliminary vote count shows ~97.92% of votes cast were in favor, representing ~67.17% of all outstanding shares
Total equity value of the transaction is approximately $10.7 billion with an enterprise value of approximately $33.4 billion including assumption of existing debt
Transaction is expected to close in late 2026 or early 2027, subject to federal, state, and foreign regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions
Proportional net debt of $22,724 million and consolidated net debt of $27,561 million as of December 31, 2025, referenced for enterprise value calculation
items.decisionTakeaway: Stockholder approval clears the key ownership-consent hurdle for the $15.00/share cash take-private, leaving regulatory approvals as the primary remaining condition to closing in late 2026 or early 2027.

keyMetrics.votesInFavor: ~97.92% of votes cast (~67.17% of outstanding shares)

items.name: HealthEquity, Inc.
items.headline: HealthEquity 2026 Annual Meeting: All Five Proposals Approved
whatChanged: Stockholders elected all ten director nominees (including CEO Scott Cutler) to serve until the 2027 Annual Meeting.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP ratified as independent auditor for fiscal year ending January 31, 2027.
Advisory say-on-pay proposal approved with 74.0M votes for vs 1.9M against.
Stockholders approved the 2026 Employee Stock Purchase Plan and the Amended and Restated 2024 Equity Incentive Plan.
83.8M shares were outstanding and entitled to vote as of the May 6, 2026 record date.
items.decisionTakeaway: All routine 2026 annual meeting proposals passed without contest, including director elections, auditor ratification, say-on-pay, and equity plan approvals, signaling no governance friction.

items.name: Visa Inc.
items.headline: Visa deposits $250M into U.S. litigation escrow, dilutes Class B conversion rates
whatChanged: Visa authorized a $250M deposit into its U.S. litigation escrow account under the U.S. retrospective responsibility plan on June 24, 2026
Class B-1 conversion rate decreased from 1.5475 to 1.5445; Class B-2 from 1.5075 to 1.5014; Class B-3 from 1.5075 to 1.4953, effective June 25, 2026
As-converted share counts reduced by ~6,658 (B-1), ~2,973 (B-2), and ~740,184 (B-3), with same EPS effect as a Class A buyback
Total as-converted Class B share reduction of approximately 749,815 shares
items.decisionTakeaway: Visa's routine $250M litigation escrow deposit triggered modest Class B conversion-rate dilution equivalent to a small share repurchase, immaterial to the $639B market cap company.

items.name: Robinhood Markets, Inc.
items.headline: Robinhood appoints Dara Bazzano as principal accounting officer
whatChanged: Board appointed Dara Bazzano as principal accounting officer effective June 25, 2026; she has served as Chief Accounting Officer since April 2026
CFO Shiv Verma ceased serving as principal accounting officer but continues as CFO and principal financial officer
Bazzano's compensation: $425K base salary, 40% target bonus, $1.54M annual equity target, ~$3.3M RSU grant vesting quarterly over two years, $400K sign-on bonus
No compensation change in connection with the principal accounting officer appointment; no related-party arrangements disclosed
items.decisionTakeaway: Robinhood formally separated the principal accounting officer role from the CFO by appointing its existing Chief Accounting Officer Dara Bazzano, a routine governance step with no change to her compensation or to CFO Shiv Verma's role.

items.name: Advance Auto Parts, Inc.
items.headline: CHRO Soler departing AAP; no replacement named
whatChanged: EVP & Chief Human Resources Officer Kristen L. Soler is leaving the Company to pursue other opportunities
Soler to serve in advisory capacity from June 26 through July 10, 2026
Soler will receive payment under the termination-without-due-cause provision of her employment agreement
No successor CHRO was named in the filing
items.decisionTakeaway: The departure of AAP's CHRO is a non-executive-officer-level management change with no named successor, unlikely to materially affect operations or outlook.

items.name: Worthington Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Q4 FY26 earnings call: margin pressure flagged as timing, data-center tanks emerging growth driver
whatChanged: Q4 adjusted EBITDA declined modestly to $83.5M from $85.1M YoY, with adjusted EPS falling to $0.97 from $1.06, driven by a ~$7M drop in ClarkDietrich equity earnings and an estimated ~$5M EBITDA headwind from lapping the A2L refrigerant transition in cooling/construction

Management characterized both headwinds as timing/normalization issues rather than structural, with A2L comparison effects expected to moderate by Q2 FY2027 and ClarkDietrich believed at a trough ($22M FY26 equity earnings, down $19M YoY)

ASME water tanks for data-center liquid cooling shipped ~$13M in FY2026 with at least that much expected in Q1 FY2027 alone; management described it as a multi-year growth platform and is investing in additional capacity
Board added Brad Southern (former Louisiana Pacific CEO) for building-products expertise; quarterly dividend raised 5% to $0.20/share
Full-year FY26: net sales $1.4B (+20%), adjusted EBITDA $295.8M (+12%), adjusted EPS $3.37 (+9% vs $3.09), free cash flow $170M (102% conversion); net debt $278M (<1x EBITDA)
items.decisionTakeaway: Q4 margin softness is tied to transient A2L comps and ClarkDietrich weakness that management calls a trough, while the emerging ASME data-center liquid-cooling tank business and 62% EBITDA growth in wholly-owned building products signal improving underlying earnings power into FY2027.

items.name: Stryker Corporation
items.headline: Stryker FY2025 results: revenue $25.1B, +11.2% YoY, EPS $8.40
whatChanged: Net sales grew 11.2% to $25,116M, with MedSurg/Neurotechnology up 16.5% and Orthopaedics up 5.4%
Adjusted diluted EPS of $13.63, up 11.8% YoY; GAAP EPS $8.40 vs $7.76 prior year
Completed Inari Medical acquisition for $4,810M net of cash; divested Spinal Implants business to Viscogliosi Brothers
Issued $3,000M in senior unsecured notes (Feb 2025) and repaid $1,400M of maturing notes; revolving credit facility increased to $3,000M
Announced Q1 2026 organizational restructuring creating new Ortho Tech business combining Mako, power tools, and enabling technologies
items.decisionTakeaway: Stryker delivered strong FY2025 growth driven by the Inari acquisition and MedSurg/Neurotechnology momentum, while the Orthopaedics segment lagged at 5.4% growth and the company undertook significant capital deployment for M&A and debt refinancing.

items.name: Allegiant Travel Company
items.headline: ALGT 2026 annual meeting: all 11 directors elected, say-on-pay and KPMG ratified
whatChanged: All 11 director nominees elected to the Board at the June 25, 2026 annual meeting
Advisory vote on executive compensation approved with 15.9M votes for vs 150K against
KPMG LLP ratified as independent auditor for FY2026 with 17.2M votes for vs 58K against
items.decisionTakeaway: Annual meeting produced no contested outcomes or governance surprises, with all directors, say-on-pay, and auditor ratification passing by wide margins.

items.name: ProAssurance Corporation
items.headline: ProAssurance merger with The Doctors Company closes; by-laws amended
whatChanged: Merger completed: Jackson Acquisition Corporation (Merger Sub, wholly owned by The Doctors Company) merged with and into ProAssurance, with ProAssurance surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary of The Doctors Company (Items 1.02, 2.01, 5.01)
ProAssurance common stock to be delisted from NYSE (Item 3.01)
Material modification to rights of security holders and change in control effected (Items 3.03, 5.01)
Director/officer changes in connection with the merger closing (Item 5.02)
Fifth Amended and Restated By-Laws adopted as part of the merger (Item 5.03, Exhibit 3.2)
items.decisionTakeaway: The previously announced acquisition of ProAssurance by The Doctors Company has closed as expected on June 26, 2026, resulting in a change of control, NYSE delisting, adoption of amended by-laws, and management changes.

items.name: Axalta Coating Systems Ltd.
items.headline: AkzoNobel-Axalta all-share merger of equals: EGM circular sets terms, Aug 5 vote
whatChanged: AkzoNobel released its EGM shareholder circular for the August 5, 2026 vote to approve the all-share merger of equals with Axalta; Axalta shareholders vote the same day
Exchange ratio fixed at 0.6539 AkzoNobel shares per Axalta share; AkzoNobel shareholders to own ~55% and Axalta shareholders ~45% of MergeCo
AkzoNobel to pay a EUR 2.5 billion Pre-Completion Distribution (special dividend) to its shareholders as a condition to closing
Combined company: ~USD 17B revenue, USD 3.3B adjusted EBITDA, USD 1.5B pro forma adjusted FCF, with ~USD 600M run-rate synergies (90% within 3 years); net leverage target 2.0x–2.5x

MergeCo governance set: CEO Poux-Guillaume (AkzoNobel), Deputy CEO Villavarayan then CFO Anderson (both Axalta), Board Chair Sachdev (Axalta), 11-director one-tier board; NYSE primary listing with Euronext Amsterdam delisting ~12 months post-close
items.decisionTakeaway: The EGM circular formalizes the merger terms and governance for the AkzoNobel-Axalta combination, setting an August 5 shareholder vote with a fixed exchange ratio of 0.6539 and a EUR 2.5B special dividend to AkzoNobel shareholders, moving the deal toward expected late-2026/early-2027 close.

keyMetrics.preCompletionDistribution: EUR 2.5 billion (special dividend to AkzoNobel shareholders)
keyMetrics.terminationFee: EUR 150 million (AkzoNobel to Axalta in certain circumstances)

items.name: Carnival Corporation Ltd.
items.headline: CCL Q2 2026: Revenue Up 5.3% YoY, EPS Misses on Higher Fuel Costs
whatChanged: Total revenue rose 5.3% YoY to $6.66B in Q2 2026, driven by higher ticket prices, onboard spending, and favorable FX, partially offset by lower air transportation revenue.
Operating income fell 8.9% YoY to $851M as a $121M increase in fuel prices and $103M nonrecurrence of prior-year ship-sale gains pressured margins, despite lower repair costs.
Net income attributable to Carnival declined to $537M ($0.39 diluted EPS) from $565M ($0.42 diluted EPS) in Q2 2025, though interest expense fell 16% to $285M due to lower debt and rates.
Carnival initiated capital returns: $414M in dividends ($0.30/share for the half-year) and $381M in share repurchases under a new $2.5B buyback program announced in March 2026.
DLC unification completed in May 2026, consolidating the dual-listed structure into a single Bermuda-incorporated entity listed solely on the NYSE, with Carnival plc shares delisted.
items.decisionTakeaway: Q2 results show continued revenue growth but margin compression from higher fuel costs and absence of prior-year ship-sale gains, while the initiation of dividends and buybacks signals management confidence in cash generation despite still-elevated leverage.

keyMetrics.totalDebt: $24.9B (net of issuance costs) vs $26.6B at Nov 30, 2025

items.name: Sila Realty Trust, Inc.
items.headline: SILA stockholders approve merger with Sunshine Ultimate Parent
whatChanged: Special Meeting held June 26, 2026: Merger Proposal approved with 34,955,162 votes for vs 325,441 against (374,073 abstentions)
Advisory Merger-Related Compensation Proposal approved with 32,797,978 for vs 2,396,291 against
Adjournment Proposal approved but deemed unnecessary; meeting was not adjourned
64.5% of outstanding shares (35,654,676 of 55,241,098) were present or represented by proxy, constituting a quorum
All three proposals passed, clearing the stockholder-approval condition for the April 19, 2026 merger agreement with Sunshine Ultimate Parent LLC
items.decisionTakeaway: Overwhelming stockholder approval (98% of cast votes) removes the key closing condition for the Sunshine merger, leaving regulatory and other customary conditions as the remaining hurdles to deal completion.

items.name: VeriSign, Inc.
items.headline: VeriSign prices $550M 5.10% senior notes due 2031
whatChanged: VeriSign entered an underwriting agreement (June 18, 2026) to issue $550,000,000 principal amount of 5.100% Senior Notes due 2031
Notes priced to underwriters at 99.361% of principal; closing scheduled for June 26, 2026
J.P. Morgan, BofA Securities, and U.S. Bancorp Investments serve as representatives of the underwriting syndicate
Notes issued under existing indenture (June 8, 2021) as supplemented by a Third Supplemental Indenture dated June 26, 2026
Net proceeds to be applied as described in the prospectus under 'Use of Proceeds'
items.decisionTakeaway: VeriSign is raising $550M of new 10-year debt at a 5.10% coupon, a modest capital raise (~2.4% of market cap) that adds leverage but does not materially alter the firm's investment profile.

items.name: Southern Copper Corporation
items.headline: SCCO secures $1.25B financing for Tía María copper project
whatChanged: SPCC secured US$1.25B in bond financing at 5.35% due 2036, primarily to develop the Tía María copper project
Tía María will add 120,000 metric tons of annual refined copper capacity, with operations expected to begin Q3 2027
Project total capital budget is US$1.8B, of which US$948M already committed; projected ~US$24.1B in exports and US$6.2B in taxes/royalties over first 20 years at current copper prices
Michiquillay project update: ~225,000 tons/year copper capacity, ~US$2.5B investment, production expected 2032
items.decisionTakeaway: SCCO has financed its next major copper growth project Tía María, which adds 120kt of annual copper capacity starting 2027 at a total capex of US$1.8B, materially extending the company's production growth runway alongside Michiquillay in 2032.

items.name: Apogee Enterprises, Inc.
items.headline: Apogee Q1 FY27 EPS $0.54 adj $0.57, reaffirms FY27 guidance
whatChanged: Q1 FY27 net sales $342.7M (-1.1% YoY); operating income $18.8M vs $6.9M (+171.8% YoY) as prior year had Fortify restructuring charges; diluted EPS $0.54 vs $(0.13) loss; adjusted diluted EPS $0.57 vs $0.56 (+1.8%)

Adjusted EBITDA declined to $32.1M (-6.6% YoY) with margin compression to 9.4% from 9.9%, driven by Architectural Glass margin falling to 8.7% from 18.3% on lower price/volume and material cost inflation
Architectural Services backlog rose to $734.5M from $682.9M at fiscal year-end; segment sales +8.2% YoY

Company reaffirmed FY27 guidance: net sales $1.38B-$1.43B, adjusted diluted EPS $2.70-$3.25; with pending Kalwall acquisition (early July close), sales range raised to $1.43B-$1.48B but EPS outlook unchanged
Returned $15.3M to shareholders ($9.7M repurchases, $5.6M dividends); long-term debt $237.4M, leverage ratio 1.3x
items.decisionTakeaway: Q1 adjusted EPS was roughly flat YoY with materially weaker Glass segment margins offset by Metals improvement and cost savings, while reaffirmed FY27 guidance and the pending Kalwall acquisition signal management confidence despite mixed segment trends.

items.name: Celldex Therapeutics, Inc.
items.headline: Celldex annual meeting approves 3.4M-share equity plan increase
whatChanged: Stockholders approved Amendment No. 4 to the 2021 Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan, increasing reserved shares by 3,400,000 to 12,900,000 total and clarifying tax withholding provisions.
All nine director nominees elected to serve until the 2027 annual meeting, with each receiving strong majority support.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP ratified as independent auditor for FY2026 (64.7M for vs 174.7K against).
Advisory say-on-pay approved (59.3M for vs 945K against).
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholders approved a routine 3.4M-share expansion of the equity incentive plan, modestly increasing potential dilution while all governance proposals passed without controversy.

items.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
items.headline: BridgeBio stockholders approve third amended equity incentive plan
whatChanged: Stockholders approved the Third Amended and Restated 2021 Stock Option and Incentive Plan on June 22, 2026, with the plan effective that date
Plan reserves 54,723,827 shares of common stock for issuance, covering all award types including incentive stock options
Non-employee director annual compensation cap set at $600,000 ($1,250,000 in a director's first calendar year)
Plan permits grants through June 25, 2029 (incentive stock options through June 21, 2029), with no repricing allowed absent stockholder approval
items.decisionTakeaway: Stockholder approval of the amended equity plan with ~54.7M reserved shares provides the company runway for equity-based compensation through mid-2029 but does not alter the fundamental investment thesis.

items.name: Wave Life Sciences Ltd.
items.headline: Shareholders overwhelmingly approve Singapore redomiciliation scheme
whatChanged: Wave Life Sciences shareholders voted to approve the Scheme of Arrangement to redomicile the company out of Singapore, with 155,190,836 shares FOR and only 78,429 AGAINST out of 155,269,265 total shares voted
All 87 voting Scheme Shareholders voted in favor of the Wave Redomiciliation Proposal
The company will next seek requisite approval from the Singapore High Court via court application; redomiciliation expected to take effect in mid-2026
The redomiciliation was previously described in the definitive proxy statement filed May 7, 2026
items.decisionTakeaway: Shareholders cleared the vote hurdle for Wave's Singapore redomiciliation with near-unanimous support, leaving only Singapore High Court approval before the expected mid-2026 effective date.

items.name: Expand Energy Corporation
items.headline: VP Accounting & Controller resigns; CFO assumes interim PAO role
whatChanged: Gregory M. Larson, VP – Accounting & Controller, notified the Company on June 23, 2026 of his intention to resign
Larson's resignation was not due to any disagreement with the Company on operations, policies, or practices
CFO Marcel Teunissen (appointed EVP & CFO on April 6, 2026) has assumed principal accounting officer duties on an interim basis
Company is conducting a search for a permanent VP – Accounting & Controller
items.decisionTakeaway: Loss of the principal accounting officer is a routine, non-disputed transition with the CFO covering interim duties, posing no immediate signal of financial reporting or governance concerns.

items.name: Jade Biosciences, Inc.
items.headline: Jade Biosciences appoints Mark Eisner to board
whatChanged: Mark Eisner, M.D., M.P.H. appointed to Board of Directors effective June 25, 2026
Dr. Eisner brings 25+ years of clinical development and immunology leadership experience
Prior roles include CMO at Vir Biotechnology, Sonoma Biotherapeutics, and FibroGen, plus ~11 years at Genentech/Roche
Appointment supports Jade's pipeline advancement in autoimmune disease therapies
items.decisionTakeaway: Jade added a seasoned immunology drug-development executive to its board as it advances its clinical-stage autoimmune pipeline including JADE101 in IgA nephropathy.

items.name: ADTRAN Holdings, Inc.
items.headline: ADTRAN appoints Anne DelSanto to board
whatChanged: Anne DelSanto appointed as independent director effective July 1, 2026
Brings 30+ years in cloud, SaaS, AI, and enterprise go-to-market from Salesforce, Oracle, IBM
Currently serves on board of Advanced Energy (AEIS); formerly on boards of Juniper Networks and New Relic
Appointment aligns with Adtran's strategic expansion into cloud infrastructure, AI-driven networking, and enterprise software
items.decisionTakeaway: New independent director with deep hyperscaler and SaaS expertise signals board-level support for Adtran's push beyond service-provider core into cloud and AI markets.
